sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: οἵαν σε μένει πυθέσθαι
sections.translation: that she , unhappy , will sing . Rather in shrill-toned odes the dirge will rise , while the hollow sound of beating hands and the shredding of grey hair will fall upon her breast .

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: φύει τ ’ ἄδηλα καὶ φανέντα κρύπτεται ·
sections.translation: Yes , better hid in Hades is the man plagued by foolishness , who by the lineage from where he springs is noblest of the enduring Achaeans , yet now is

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: πρὸς τἡςδε τῆς γυναικός · οἰκτείρω δὲ νιν
sections.translation: constant no more in his inbred temperament , but wanders outside himself . O Telamon , unhappy father , how heavy a curse upon your son awaits your hearing , a curse which never yet has

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: μολών τε , χῶρον ἔνθ ’ ἂν ἀστιβῆ κίχω ,
sections.translation: any life-portion of the heirs of Aeacus nourished but his !

sections.label: Section 646
sections.original: κρύψω τόδ ’ ἔγχος τοὐμὸν , ἔχθιστον βελῶν ,
sections.translation: All things the long and countless years first draw from darkness , and then bury from light ; and there is nothing which man should not expect : the dread power of oath is conquered , as is unyielding will .

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: παρ ’ Ἕκτορος δώρημα δυσμενεστάτου ,
sections.translation:
For even I , who used to be so tremendously strong — yes , like tempered iron — felt my tongue ’ s sharp edge emasculated by this woman ’ s words , and I feel the pity of leaving her a widow and the boy an orphan among my enemies .
But I will go to the bathing-place and

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: εἴκειν , μαθησόμεσθα δ ’ Ἀτρείδας σέβειν .
sections.translation: the meadows by the shore so that by purging my defilements I may escape the heavy anger of the goddess . Then I will find some isolated spot , and bury this sword of mine , most hateful weapon , digging down in the earth where none can see .

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: ἐξίσταται δὲ νυκτὸς αἰανὴς κύκλος
sections.translation: Let Night and Hades keep it underground ! For ever since I took into my hand this gift from Hector , my greatest enemy , I have gotten no good from the Greeks . Yes , men ’ s proverb is true :

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δὲ πῶς οὐ γνωσόμεσθα σωφρονεῖν ;
sections.translation:
the gifts of enemies are no gifts and bring no good .
And so hereafter I shall , first , know how to yield to the gods , and , second , learn to revere the Atreidae . They are rulers , so we must submit . How could it be otherwise ? Things of awe and might

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: ὡς αἰὲν οὐ μενοῦντα · τοῖς πολλοῖσι γὰρ
sections.translation: submit to authority . So it is that winter with its snow-covered paths gives place to fruitful summer ; night ’ s dark orbit makes room for day with her white horses to kindle her radiance ; the blast of dreadful winds

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: ὑμεῖς θ , ἑταῖροι , ταὐτὰ τῇδέ μοι τάδε
sections.translation:
allows the groaning sea to rest ; and among them all , almighty Sleep releases the fettered sleeper , and does not hold him in a perpetual grasp .
And we men — must we not learn self-restraint ? I , at least , will learn it , since I am newly aware that an enemy is to be hated only as far as

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: πύθοισθε , κεἰ νῦν δυστυχῶ , σεσωσμένον .
sections.translation:
suits one who will in turn become a friend . Similarly to a friend I would wish to give only so much help and service as suits him who will not forever remain friendly . For the masses regard the haven of comradeship as treacherous .
But concerning these things it will be well . You , wife ,

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: φάνηθ ’, ὦ θεῶν χοροποί ’ ἄναξ ,
sections.translation:
go inside and pray to the gods that the desires of my heart be completed to the very end . Exit Tecmessa .
You also , my comrades , honor my wishes just as she does , and command Teucer , when he comes , to take care of us , and to be kind to you at the same time .

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: μολὼν ἄναξ Ἀπόλλων
sections.translation: I am going to where my journey inexorably leads . But you do as I say , and before long , perhaps , though I now suffer , you will hear that I have found rest and peace .

sections.label: Section 693
sections.original: ἔλυσε γὰρ αἰνὸν ἄχος ἀπ ’ ὀμμάτων Ἄρης .
sections.translation: I shiver with rapture ; I soar on the wings of sudden joy !

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: νῦν , ὦ Ζεῦ , πάρα λευκὸν εὐάμερον πελάσαι φάος
sections.translation: O Pan , O Pan , appear to us , sea-rover , from the stony ridge of snow-beaten Cyllene . King , dancemaker for the gods , come , so that joining with us you may set on the Nysian and the Cnosian steps ,

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: πάνθ ’ ὁ μέγας χρόνος μαραίνει τε καὶ φλέγει ,
sections.translation: your self-taught dances . Now I want to dance . And may Apollo , lord of Delos , step over the Icarian sea

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: Ἄνδρες φίλοι , τὸ πρῶτον ἀγγεῖλαι θέλω ,
sections.translation: and join me in his divine form , in eternal benevolence !

sections.label: Section 707
sections.original: κρημνῶν · μέσον δὲ προςμολὼν στρατήγιον
sections.translation: Ares has dispelled the cloud of fierce trouble from our eyes . Joy , joy ! Now , Zeus , now can the white radiance of prosperous days approach

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: μαθόντες ἀμφέστησαν , εἶτ ’ ὀνείδεσιν
sections.translation:
our swift , sea-speeding ships , since Ajax forgets his pain anew , and has instead fully performed all prescribed sacrifices to the gods with worship and strict observance .
The strong years make all things fade .

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: ὥστ ’ ἐς τοσοῦτον ἦλθον , ὥστε καὶ χεροῖν
sections.translation: And so I would not say that anything was beyond belief , when beyond our hopes , Ajax has been converted from his fury and mighty struggles against the Atreidae .

sections.label: Section 719
sections.original: ἀλλ ἡμὶν Αἴας ποῦ ’ στιν , ὡς φράσω τάδε ;
sections.translation: Friends , my first news is this :

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: τοῖς κυρίοις γὰρ πάντα χρὴ δηλοῦν λόγον ,
sections.translation: Teucer has just now returned from the Mysian heights . He has come to the generals ’ quarters mid-camp , and is being shouted at by all the Greeks at once . Recognizing him from a distance as he approached , they gathered around him

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: πέμπων ἔπεμψεν , ἢ ’ φάνην ἐγὼ βραδύς ;
sections.translation: and then pelted him with jeers from every side — no one held back — calling him the brother of the maniac , of the plotter against the army , and saying that he would not be able to avoid entirely losing flesh and life before their flying stones . In this way they had come to the point where swords

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: γνώμης , θεοῖσιν ὡς καταλλαχθῇ χόλου .
sections.translation: had been plucked from sheaths and were drawn in their hands . But then the conflict , when it had nearly run its full course , was halted by the conciliatory words of the elders . But where shall I find Ajax , to tell him this ? To our lord I must tell all .

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: ἐκ γὰρ ξυνέδρου καὶ τυραννικοῦ κύκλου
sections.translation: He is not inside , but is recently departed . He has yoked a new purpose to his new mood .

sections.label: Section 737
sections.original: ἐς χεῖρα Τεύκρου δεξιὰν φιλοφρόνως
sections.translation: No ! Oh , no ! Too late , then , was he who sent me on this errand , or I myself came too slowly .

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: Αἴανθ ’ ὑπὸ σκηναῖσι , μηδ ’ ἀφέντ ’ ὲᾷν ,
sections.translation: What is this urgent matter ? What part of it has been neglected ?

sections.label: Section 741
sections.original:
755
εἰ ζῶντ ’ ἐκεῖνον εἰςιδεἷν θέλοι ποτέ .
sections.translation: Teucer declared that Ajax should not slip out of the house , until he himself arrives .

sections.label: Section 743
sections.original: δίας Ἀθανᾶς μῆνις , ὡς ἔφη λέγων .
sections.translation: Well , he is departed , I repeat , bent on the purpose that is best for him — to be rid of his anger at the gods .

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: πίπτειν βαρείαις πρὸς θεῶν δυςπραξίαις
sections.translation: These words betray great foolishness , if there is any wisdom in the prophecies of Calchas .

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: βλαστὼν , ἔπειτα μὴ κατ ’ ἄνθρωπον φρονεῖ .
sections.translation: What does he prophesy ? What knowledge of this affair do you bring ?

sections.label: Section 748
sections.original: κεῖνος δ ’ ἀπ ’ οἴκων εὐθὺς ἐξορμώμενος
sections.translation: This much I know and witnessed on the spot . Leaving the royal circle of the chiefs who sat in council ,

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: ὁ μὲν γὰρ αὐτὸν ἐννέπει , τέκνον , δορὶ
sections.translation: Calchas separated himself from the Atreidae and put his right hand with all kindness into the hand of Teucer . The prophet then addressed him and strictly commanded him to use every possible resource to keep Ajax inside his tent for the duration of this day that now shines on us , and to prevent him from moving about

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: κείνων πέποιθα τοῦτ ’ ἐπισπάσειν κλέος .
sections.translation: if he wished ever to look on him alive . For this day alone will the anger of divine Athena lash at him . That was the prophet ’ s warning . Yes , the seer went on to explain , lives that have grown too proud and no longer yield good fall on grave difficulties sent from the gods ,

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: Ἄνασσα , τοῖς ἄλλοισιν Ἀργέων πέλας
sections.translation: especially when someone born to man ’ s estate forgets that fact by thinking thoughts too high for man . And Ajax , even at the time he first set out from home , showed himself foolish , when his father advised him well . For Telamon told him , My son , seek victory in arms , but always seek it with the help of god . Then with a tall boast and foolishly he replied , Father , with the help of the gods even a worthless man might achieve victory ; but I , even without that help , fully trust to bring that glory within my grasp .

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: ὦ δαΐα Τέκμησσα , δύςιιορον γένος ,
sections.translation: So much he boasted . Then once again in answer to divine Athena — at a time when she was urging him forward and telling him to turn a deadly hand against the enemy — he answered her with words terrible and blasphemous , Queen , stand beside the other Greeks ; where Ajax stands , battle will never break our line . It was by such words , you must know , that he won for himself the intolerable anger of the goddess since his thoughts were too high for man . But if he survives this day , perhaps with the god ’ s help we may find means to save him .

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: Καὶ μὴν θυραῖος , ὥστε μ ’ ὠδίνειν τί φής ;
sections.translation: With those words the seer finished , and at once Teucer rose from his seat and sent me with these orders for you to follow . But if I have been cheated of success , Ajax does not live . Otherwise Calchas has no skill .

sections.label: Section 784
sections.original: Πάρεστ ’ ἐκεῖνος ἄρτι · τήνδε δ ’ ἔξοδον
sections.translation: Poor Tecmessa , born to misery ,

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: ὀλεθρίαν Αἴαντος ἐλπίζει φέρειν .
sections.translation: come out and see this man and his news . The razor lies close at our throat , poised to cut off all joy .

sections.label: Section 787
sections.original: Τοῦ Θεστορεἰου μάντεως , καθ ’ ἡμέραν
sections.translation: Why do you stir me from my place of rest , when I have just found peace from those relentless troubles ?

sections.label: Section 789
sections.original: Οἴ ’ γὼ , φίλοι , πρόστητ ’ ἀναγκαίας τύχης ·
sections.translation: Listen to this man , and

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: καὶ σπεύσαθ ’, οἱ μὲν Τεῦκρον ὲν τάχει μολεῖν ,
sections.translation: hear the news of Ajax that he has brought us — news at which I felt sudden grief .

sections.label: Section 791
sections.original:
805
οἱ δ ’ ἑσπέρους ἀγκῶνας , οἵ δ ’ ἀντηλίους
sections.translation: Oh , no , what is your news , man ? Surely we are not ruined ?

sections.label: Section 792
sections.original: ζητεῖτ ’ ἰόντες τἀνδρὸς ἔξοδον κακήν .
sections.translation: I have no clue of your condition , but know only that , if Ajax is away , I have little hope for him .

sections.label: Section 794
sections.original: καὶ τῆς παλαιᾶς χάριτος ἐκβεβλημένη .
sections.translation: But he is away , so I am in agony to know what you mean .

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: οἴ μοι , τί δράσω , τέκνον ; οὐχ ἱδρυτέον .
sections.translation: Teucer strictly commands that you keep Ajax under shelter of his tent and not allow him to go out alone .

sections.label: Section 797
sections.original: χωρῶμεν , ἐγκονῶμεν , οὐχ ἕδρας ἀκμὴ ,
sections.translation: But where is Teucer ? And why these orders ?

sections.label: Section 798
sections.original: σώζειν θέλοντες ἄνδρα γ ’ ὅς σπεύδει θανεῖν .
sections.translation: He has just now returned , and he suspects that such a departure carries death for Ajax .

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: τάχος γὰρ ἔργου καὶ ποδῶν ἄμ ’ ἕψεται .
sections.translation: Oh , misery ! From whom can he have learned this ?

sections.label: Section 801
sections.original:
815
Ὁ μὲν σφαγεὺς ἕστηκεν , ᾗ τομώτατος
sections.translation: From Thestor ’ s son , the prophet . His prophecy applies to today , when the issue is one of life or death for Ajax .

sections.label: Section 803
sections.original: δῶρον μὲν ἀνὁρὸς Ἕκτορος ξένων ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: Ah , me ! My friends , protect me from the doom threatened by fate ! Hurry , some of you , to speed Teucer ’ s coming ;

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: πέπηγε δ ’ ὲν γῇ πολεμίᾳ τῇ Τρῳάδι
sections.translation: let others go to the westward bays , and others to the eastward , and there seek the man ’ s disastrous path . I see now that I have been deceived by my husband and cast out of the favor that I once had with him . Ah , my child , what shall I do ? I must not sit idle .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: σὺ πρῶτος , ὦ Ζεῦ , καὶ γὰρ εἰκὸς , ἄρκεσον .
sections.translation: I too will go as far as my strength will carry me . Move , let us be quick , this is no time to sit still , if we wish to save a man who is eager for death .

sections.label: Section 813
sections.original: Τεύκρῳ φέροντα , πρῶτος ὥς με βαστάσῃ
sections.translation: I am ready to help , and I will show it in more than word . Speed of action and speed of foot will follow together .

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: καὶ μὴ πρὸς ἐχθρῶν του κατοπτευθεὶς πάρος
sections.translation: The sacrificial killer stands planted in the way that will cut most deeply — if I have the leisure for even this much reflection . First , it is the gift of Hector , that enemy-friend who was most hateful to me and most hostile to my sight ; next , it is fixed in enemy soil , the land of Troy ,

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: πλευρὰν διαρρήξαντα τώδε φασγάνῳ .
sections.translation:
newly-whetted on the iron-devouring stone ; and finally I have planted it with scrupulous care , so that it should prove most kind to me by a speedy death .
Yes , we are well equipped . And so , O Zeus , be the first to aid me , as is proper .

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: καί σφας κακοὺς κάκιστα καὶ πανωλέθρους
sections.translation: It is no large prize that I ask you to award me . Send on my behalf some messenger with news of my downfall to Teucer , so that he may be the first to raise me once I have fallen on this sword and made it newly-wet , and so that I am not first spotted by some enemy

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: γεύεσθε , μὴ φείδεσθε πανδήμου στρατοῦ .
sections.translation: and cast out and exposed as prey to the dogs and birds . For this much , Zeus , I appeal to you . I call also on Hermes , guide to the underworld , to lay me softly to sleep with one quick , struggle-free leap , when I have broken open my side on this sword .

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: γέροντι πατρὶ , τῇ τε δυστήνῳ τροφῷ .
sections.translation: And I call for help to the eternal maidens who eternally attend to all sufferings among mortals , the dread , far-striding Erinyes , asking them to learn how my miserable life is destroyed by the Atreidae .
